    "Gate failure on mandate-band compliance: post-action equity exposure 81.2% breaches the 60-70% mandate band by 11.2 percentage points; the proposed Rs 2 Cr fresh inflow adds incremental equity without any offsetting rebalance, widening an already-breached band (pre-action 72.2% already 2.2pp above ceiling)",
    "Gate failure on wrapper-count discipline: post-action PMS count rises to five discrete PMS mandates; wrapper-aggregate exposure moves from approximately 39% to approximately 45% of advisory liquid AUM; no corresponding exit is proposed, compounding operational and oversight complexity",
    "Manager data thinness: disclosed AUM Rs 33.52 Cr is materially small for a thematic PMS; two named managers (Umesh Gupta, Ashutosh Garud) with no disclosed tenure, prior-vintage track record, or team depth available in the snapshot; E6 cannot assess manager continuity or succession risk from available data",
    "Strategy data thinness: only one return data point available (1Y: 5.65% vs Nifty 50 TRI benchmark); no 3-year, 5-year, or since-inception series disclosed; single-year return of 5.65% against a broad equity benchmark is insufficient to assess strategy consistency, style stability, or drawdown behaviour across regimes",
    "Fee structure: fixed AMC 1.75% + 10% profit sharing above 12% hurdle; no high-water-mark mechanism disclosed in snapshot; absence of HWM confirmation is a structural concern for performance-fee fairness across drawdown-recovery cycles",
    "Fee normalisation: blended cost estimate over 5-year horizon — fixed AMC 1.75% + estimated performance fee contribution approximately 0.6% (10% of returns above 12% hurdle; assuming 3 of 5 years clear hurdle at modest margin given 1Y return of 5.65%) + estimated transaction/custody costs approximately 0.3% = approximately 265 bps blended; comparable infrastructure/capex-themed MF (e.g., DSP India T.I.G.E.R. Fund or ICICI Pru Infrastructure Fund, direct plan) runs approximately 90-110 bps; PMS premium approximately 155-175 bps; premium is not justified by available alpha evidence given single data point of 5.65% 1Y return",
    "Capacity signal: AUM of Rs 33.52 Cr is very low for a thematic PMS; while low AUM reduces capacity-ceiling concern, it raises questions about institutional traction, operational maturity, and whether the strategy has been stress-tested at meaningful scale",
    "Thematic overlap and concentration risk: infrastructure/capital-goods thematic PMS adds a sector-concentrated overlay on top of four existing PMS mandates (quality-concentrated, growth-quality, value, focused-midcap); the existing four mandates already carry capital-goods and infrastructure exposure as incidental holdings; incremental thematic concentration in a cyclical sector is additive risk without disclosed diversification benefit",
    "Peer-referral provenance: investor rationale is anchored on a three-year peer experience in a favourable infrastructure cycle (2022-2025 capex upcycle); this is a recency-and-social-proof pattern, not an analytical basis; Aanchal Bhatt's documented skepticism is noted as a family-level dissenting signal"

  "reasoning_paragraph": "The proposed Rs 2 Cr allocation to Ambit Build India Portfolio (PMS) fails the Gate on two independent structural grounds before product-level analysis is reached: first, post-action equity exposure of 81.2% breaches the 60-70% mandate band by 11.2 percentage points, compounding a pre-existing 2.2pp breach rather than resolving it; second, the wrapper count rises to five PMS mandates with wrapper-aggregate exposure at approximately 45% of advisory liquid AUM, a level that introduces meaningful oversight and operational complexity without a corresponding rebalance or exit. At the product level, the available data is materially thin: only a single 1-year return of 5.65% is disclosed, with no 3-year, 5-year, or since-inception series; manager tenure and team depth are not available in the snapshot; and AUM of Rs 33.52 Cr raises questions about institutional traction and operational maturity at scale. The fee structure of 1.75% fixed AMC plus 10% profit sharing above a 12% hurdle normalises to approximately 265 bps blended over a 5-year horizon, representing a premium of approximately 155-175 bps over comparable infrastructure-themed MF alternatives; this premium is not supportable from the single available return data point, which itself trails the hurdle rate. The absence of a disclosed high-water-mark mechanism is a structural concern for performance-fee fairness across drawdown-recovery cycles. The infrastructure/capital-goods thematic overlay adds cyclical sector concentration on top of four existing PMS mandates that already carry incidental capital-goods exposure, without a disclosed diversification rationale. The investor's stated rationale — a peer's three-year positive experience during a favourable capex upcycle — reflects a recency-and-social-proof pattern rather than an analytical basis; the family-level dissenting signal from Aanchal Bhatt is noted. Taken together, the Gate failures, data insufficiency, fee premium without alpha coverage, and mandate-architecture deterioration place this proposal at high risk level.",
